title: podman push show  Digest did not match in rootless mode
body:
### Issue Description

in rootless mode , we push a  image to harbor, it always failed by   ```Digest did not match```, but we use root user to push a image  , it always normal



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. create a shared images in root user dir , like /opt/containers/pub_img
2. set /etc/containers/storage.con
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/opt/containers/admin"
imagestore = "/opt/containers/pub_img"
```
3. cd /opt/containers && podman pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest
4.  chmod -R a+rx /opt/containers/pub_img
5.  adduser test-user
6. set test-user storage config
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"

graphroot = "/opt/containers/users/test-user/root"
imagestore = "/opt/containers/pri_img/test-user"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = ["/opt/containers/pub_img"]

[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```
8. sudo -u test-user podman run -it -d --name=x3 --network=none docker.io/library/busybox
9. sudo -u test-user podman commit -p -f docker -a "test-user" -m "test-user" x3 192.168.10.111/test-user/busybox:v1
10. sudo -u test-user podman  --log-level trace push  192.168.10.111/test-user/busybox:v1

### Describe the results you received

Error: writing blob: Patch "http:// 192.168.10.111/v2/test-user/busybox/blobs/uploads/5180bdd1-086d-42ab-8718-5d0eb7dec6a2?_state=4t9QqeZ1QtYby9Zm4jHcd-u8mMDENCGzh0slTa2Eqcx7Ik5hbWUiOiJjdXN0b20vYnVzeWJveCIsIlVVSUQiOiI1MTgwYmRkMS0wODZkLTQyYWItODcxOC01ZDBlYjdkZWM2YTIiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjAsIlN0YXJ0ZWRBdCI6IjIwMjUtMDctMjVUMDY6MzM6NTUuMzA4MDQ5OTgzWiJ9": happened during read: Digest did not match, expected sha256:65014c70e84b6817fac42bb201ec5c1ea460a8da246cac0e481f5c9a9491eac0, got sha256:31c1c6b9e0bb5e2d1233de6106ca71c997099903025b2c103d2a6ffcd89d5218

### Describe the results you expected

push successfully

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
> > `chmod -R a+rx /opt/containers/pub_img`
> 
> That kind of a manual interference is certainly not expected. There is a `force_mask` option in `storage.conf` that is probably applicable, I’m not sure whether there is more to such a use case. storage.conf `storage.conf` `force_mask` 
> 
> [@giuseppe](https://github.com/giuseppe) ?

same question, and set force_mask at /etc/containers/storage.conf 
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/opt/containers/pubimg"

[storage.options]
[storage.options.overlay]
force_mask = "shared"
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```

then use root user pull image
`podman pull quay.io/travelping/nettools:v1.12.0`

The directory permissions are like this
```
ll /opt/containers/pubimg
total 152
drwxr-xr-x 8 root root   4096 Jul 25 22:46 ./
drwxr-xr-x 9 root root   4096 Jul 25 22:46 ../
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 114688 Jul 25 22:46 db.sql
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root      8 Jul 25 22:46 defaultNetworkBackend
drwx------ 2 root root   4096 Jul 25 22:46 libpod/
drwx------ 8 root root   4096 Jul 25 22:47 overlay/
drwx------ 2 root root   4096 Jul 25 22:46 overlay-containers/
drwx------ 3 root root   4096 Jul 25 22:47 overlay-images/
drwx------ 2 root root   4096 Jul 25 22:47 overlay-layers/
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root     64 Jul 25 22:47 storage.lock
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root      0 Jul 25 22:46 userns.lock
drwx------ 2 root root   4096 Jul 25 22:46 volumes/

ll /opt/containers/pubimg/overlay-images/
total 20
drwx------ 3 root root 4096 Jul 25 22:47 ./
drwxr-xr-x 8 root root 4096 Jul 25 22:46 ../
drwx------ 2 root root 4096 Jul 25 22:47 d9894787f413ba50e01b406925e0f99c819efe0009e27e3980301c9713c01c31/
-rw------- 1 root root 1199 Jul 25 22:47 images.json
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root   64 Jul 25 22:47 images.lock
```

and then use rootless user 
```
sudo -u p0ro24eo podman image ls
Error: configure storage: open /opt/containers/pubimg/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied
```

still need to chmod -R a+rx to let rootless use to read public image

> > `chmod -R a+rx /opt/containers/pub_img`
> 
> That kind of a manual interference is certainly not expected. There is a `force_mask` option in `storage.conf` that is probably applicable, I’m not sure whether there is more to such a use case.

FYI that was "documented" by us, i.e. https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/image-stores-podman, and I have seen it mentione din other palces

> That kind of a manual interference is certainly not expected. There is a `force_mask` option in `storage.conf` that is probably applicable, I’m not sure whether there is more to such a use case.

we've configured force_mask option also, but it appears to have no effect. does anyone have any suggestions or insights？

I believe the essence of the problem lies in the fact that while the `additionalimagestores` parameter is provided to allow rootless users to read shared images, but there is no option to control the permissions  when pulling images to the shared image directory，The expected permissions are  files 0644/directories 0755 .  I found the relevant previous issue, but there seems to be no solution.
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20826
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20868
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
